info.title: ai& API
info.description: Unified spec covering inference, files, and other public surfaces. Drop-in compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic SDKs.

get.description: Lists all active models with pricing.

post.description: Creates a chat completion for the provided messages and parameters.

requestBody.description: The request body for the chat completion

post.description: Creates a completion for the provided prompt and parameters. This is a legacy endpoint — use /v1/chat/completions for new integrations.

requestBody.description: The request body for the completion

post.description: Creates a response for the provided input and parameters.

requestBody.description: The request body for the response.
The file bytes plus optional purpose hint

get.description: Returns files in reverse-chronological order (newest first).
Returns metadata for a single file.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the file row and best-effort removes the underlying R2 object.

get.description: Returns the raw bytes with `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition` headers set from the upload metadata.

post.description: Creates a chunked upload session for files larger than the single-shot `/v1/files` cap. Sessions expire 1 hour after creation.

post.summary: Add a part to an upload
post.description: Uploads up to 64 MB of bytes as one part of a pending session. Parts must be added in order — completing the upload submits them in the order returned to the client.
Assembles the listed parts in order and materializes the upload as a regular `file-...` object referenced via the returned `file` field.
Aborts the underlying R2 multipart upload and marks the session `cancelled`. Idempotent — already-completed or already-cancelled sessions return 404.

model.description: ID of the model to use for the chat completion.

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far.

stream.description: If true, partial message deltas will be sent as server-sent events.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Higher values make output more random.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling: consider tokens with top_p probability mass.

n.description: How many chat completion choices to generate.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate. Deprecated in favor of max_completion_tokens.

max_completion_tokens.description: Upper bound for tokens that can be generated, including visible output and reasoning tokens.

frequency_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize tokens based on existing frequency.

presence_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far.

logprobs.description: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens.

top_logprobs.description: Number of most likely tokens to return at each position (0-20). Requires logprobs=true.

logit_bias.description: Map of token IDs to bias values (-100 to 100) to modify likelihood of specified tokens.

seed.description: If specified, the system will attempt deterministic sampling. Determinism is not guaranteed.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call.

tool_choice.description: Controls which tool is called. "none", "auto", "required", or a specific tool object.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.

reasoning_effort.description: Constrains reasoning effort for reasoning models. One of: none, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh.

top_k.description: Number of highest probability tokens to keep for top-k sampling. -1 to disable.

min_p.description: Minimum probability threshold relative to the top token. Tokens below this are filtered out. 0.0 to disable.

repetition_penalty.description: Penalizes new tokens based on whether they appear in the generated text so far. Values > 1.0 penalize repetitions.

min_tokens.description: Minimum number of tokens to generate before allowing a stop condition.

echo.description: If true, the prompt is prepended to the generated output.

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user.

model.description: ID of the model to use for the completion.

prompt.description: The prompt(s) to generate completions for.

stream.description: If true, partial completions will be sent as server-sent events.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling: consider tokens with top_p probability mass.

n.description: How many completions to generate for each prompt.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

frequency_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on existing frequency in the text so far.

presence_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far.

logprobs.description: Include the log probabilities on the most likely output tokens (0-5).

echo.description: Echo back the prompt in addition to the completion.

best_of.description: Generates best_of completions server-side and returns the best one. Cannot be used with stream.

suffix.description: The suffix that comes after the completion.

seed.description: If specified, the system will attempt deterministic sampling.

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user.

model.description: ID of the model to use for this response.

input.description: Text, image, or file inputs to the model. Can be a string or an array of input items.

instructions.description: A system (or developer) message inserted into the model's context.

stream.description: If true, response data will be streamed as server-sent events.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Higher values make output more random.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling: consider tokens with top_p probability mass.

max_output_tokens.description: Upper bound for tokens that can be generated, including visible output and reasoning tokens.

tools.description: An array of function tools the model may call while generating a response.

tool_choice.description: How the model should select which tool to use. "none", "auto", "required", or a specific tool.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow the model to run tool calls in parallel.

reasoning.description: Configuration for reasoning models: effort level and summary format.

truncation.description: "auto" truncates input to fit context window. "disabled" (default) returns 400 if input exceeds context.

previous_response_id.description: ID of a previous response to continue a multi-turn conversation.

store.description: Whether to store the response for later retrieval via API.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for additional information.

text.description: Configuration for text response format (text, json_object, json_schema).

top_logprobs.description: Number of most likely tokens to return at each position (0-20).

seed.description: If specified, the system will attempt deterministic sampling.

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens.

top_k.description: Number of highest probability tokens to keep for top-k sampling. -1 to disable.

repetition_penalty.description: Penalizes new tokens based on whether they appear in the generated text so far. Values > 1.0 penalize repetitions.

min_tokens.description: Minimum number of tokens to generate before allowing a stop condition.

ResponseInput.description: Text, image, or file inputs to the model. Can be a string (treated as user message) or an array of input items.

status.description: The status of the response generation.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this response was created.

completed_at.description: Unix timestamp of completion. Only present when status is completed.

model.description: The model used to generate the response.

output.description: An array of content items generated by the model.

error.description: Error details if the response generation failed.

incomplete_details.description: Details about why the response is incomplete.

usage.description: Token usage details for this response.

purpose.description: One of `vision`, `video`, `audio`, or `document`. Determines size + mime limits and which models can reference the file. Optional — if omitted, inferred from the file's MIME type.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp; defaults to 30 days from upload

deleted.description: Always true on a successful delete

id.description: Stable upload session id with `upload-` prefix

bytes.description: Declared total size of the file being uploaded

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp (1 hour from creation by default)

bytes.description: Declared total size in bytes; checked against `purpose` limits

data.description: Bytes for this part (multipart/form-data field), max 64 MB

part_ids.description: Ordered list of part ids returned by /parts

md5.description: Optional MD5 of the assembled file
